title: Israel x Hezbollah ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between Israel and Hezbollah by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the Israel and Hezbollah, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the Israeli Government and Hezbollah. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A U.S.-brokered 10-day cessation of hostilities between Israel and Lebanon took effect on April 16, 2026, halting intense exchanges of Israeli airstrikes and Hezbollah rocket fire that intensified since early March, amid over 2,000 Lebanese casualties reported. The agreement, excluding Hezbollah formally but urging compliance, enables first direct Israel-Lebanon talks in decades toward a permanent security deal, with President Trump hailing it as historic and Netanyahu viewing it as a peace opportunity. Despite sporadic explosions and minor violations in southern Lebanon, the truce holds as of April 19, but traders eye fragility—potential extension by April 26 hinges on negotiation progress in an uncertain diplomatic window, amid broader U.S.-Iran talks.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain edges France as the slim trader favorite at 16.0% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup following the completion of all 48-team qualification on April 1, with Iraq securing the final spot via playoffs, solidifying an expanded field ripe for upsets. Spain's flawless European qualification run, bolstered by Euro 2024 success and Lamine Yamal's emergence, drives their narrow lead, while France's consistent finals appearances and Kylian Mbappé-led attack hold them at 15.9%, keeping the top tightly contested. England (10.9%), Argentina (8.8%), and Brazil (8.5%) cluster closely amid squad depth advantages offset by historical inconsistencies, revenge factors, and the chaotic 48-team format's added matches and variance, reflecting balanced group draws and no dominant powerhouse.

title: Rockets vs. Lakers
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 18 at 8:30PM ET:
If the Rockets win, the market will resolve to "Rockets".
If the Lakers win, the market will resolve to "Lakers".

eventMetadata.context_description: In the wake of the Lakers' surprising 107-98 Game 1 playoff victory over the Rockets on April 18, trader sentiment hinges on persistent injury concerns and home-court edge for Los Angeles in Game 2 on April 21. The Lakers prevailed without Luka Doncic (left hamstring strain, out) and Austin Reaves (left oblique strain, out), fueled by Luke Kennard's playoff career-high 27 points off the bench and LeBron James' 19 points with 13 assists, exposing Houston's vulnerabilities. Rockets remain sidelined by Steven Adams (ankle) and Fred VanVleet (ACL), with Kevin Durant questionable (right knee contusion); their late-season eight-game win streak offers momentum, but LA's rest advantage and Crypto.com Arena crowd could sustain the series lead amid thin rosters on both sides.

eventMetadata.context_description: In Game 1 of their Eastern Conference first-round playoff series at TD Garden, the Boston Celtics hold a significant edge as the No. 2 seed with a clean injury report, confirming full availability for Jayson Tatum, Jaylen Brown, Derrick White, and the rest of their rotation following recent management of Achilles and knee issues. The No. 7 seed Philadelphia 76ers, however, face a major setback without Joel Embiid, out indefinitely due to appendectomy recovery, forcing Tyrese Maxey to shoulder a heavier load against Boston's elite defense. Recent 76ers play-in resilience propelled them here, but Celtics' home-court advantage, superior regular-season form, and rest edge shape trader consensus toward the hosts in this high-stakes rivalry opener.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder command 47.5% implied probability as 2026 NBA Championship favorites, fueled by their league-best 64-18 record, No. 1 Western Conference seed, defending 2025 title, elite defense, and MVP frontrunner Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's return from minor ailments. San Antonio Spurs sit at 14.4% after a 62-20 surge, including an 11-game win streak capped by Victor Wembanyama's dominance en route to the No. 2 West seed facing Portland in the first round. Boston Celtics hold 12.7% as East No. 2 (56-26), while Denver Nuggets' 12 straight wins to claim No. 3 West boosts them to 9.5%. Playoffs tipped off April 18 with early favorites advancing in Game 1s like Nuggets over Timberwolves, underscoring OKC's path through a familiar West gauntlet.

title: Counter-Strike: Spirit vs Vitality (BO5) - IEM Rio Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Spirit and Vitality in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 19 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit" if Spirit win the match against Vitality.

This market will resolve to "Vitality" if Vitality win the match against Spirit.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Spirit and Team Vitality advanced to the IEM Rio 2026 grand final after dominant playoff runs, with Spirit sweeping MOUZ 2-0 and Falcons 2-0 in quarters, while Vitality dispatched Natus Vincere 2-0 and FURIA 2-0 amid tight maps on Overpass and Ancient. Vitality, world #1, chases a historic second Intel Grand Slam behind ZywOo's 1.27 rating in semis, bolstered by ropz and flameZ's clutches, though they stumbled 1-2 to Falcons in groups. Spirit (#5) rides donk's aggressive entries and a tougher group stage path over G2 and Liquid, with stable rosters featuring magixx, sh1ro, and zont1x. Head-to-head favors Vitality historically, but BO5 map veto—Mirage and Dust2 for Spirit, Nuke and Overpass for Vitality—sets up a stylistic clash in Rio's Farmasi Arena.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions California Governor Gavin Newsom as the clear frontrunner for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 27.5%, buoyed by his executive experience, national media profile from challenging Trump-era policies, and strong fundraising amid a post-2024 reset. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.3% on progressive grassroots appeal and youth, while Kamala Harris sits at 6.8% despite some national polls showing her ahead, reflecting voter fatigue from her 2024 defeat. Jon Ossoff (6.5%) and Pete Buttigieg (4.2%) draw support as battleground senators and polished communicators, respectively. Consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm results for governors like Josh Shapiro and Gretchen Whitmer, party endorsements, and unifying moderate-progressive divides before Iowa caucuses and primaries.

title: Magic vs. Pistons
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 19 at 6:30PM ET:
If the Magic win, the market will resolve to "Magic".
If the Pistons win, the market will resolve to "Pistons".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: In Game 1 of the Eastern Conference first-round playoffs, top-seeded Detroit Pistons (60-22) host the No. 8 Orlando Magic (45-37) at Little Caesars Arena, where home-court advantage and a healthy injury report—no players listed out—bolster Detroit's edge after a week of focused practice. Orlando advanced via a 121-90 play-in rout of Charlotte on April 17, building momentum from their 123-107 regular-season win over Detroit on April 6 behind Paolo Banchero's 31 points and Desmond Bane's 25. Magic forward Jonathan Isaac remains questionable with a left knee sprain, though Wendell Carter Jr. is available; Cade Cunningham leads a physical Pistons squad aiming to leverage superior regular-season form and rest.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder host the Phoenix Suns in Game 1 of their Western Conference first-round playoff series, with OKC holding top-seed home-court advantage after clinching the No. 1 spot at 64-18. The Thunder benefit from over 11 days' rest, featuring reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's dominant form against Phoenix's perimeter defenders like Dillon Brooks, bolstered by an elite defense and deep rotation. Suns punched their No. 8 ticket via Thursday's play-in win over Golden State, arriving on short rest, with Grayson Allen (left hamstring strain) and Mark Williams (left foot soreness) questionable per official injury report—potentially straining depth. Despite Phoenix's 135-103 rout of a rested OKC in the April 12 finale, OKC's superior regular-season dominance and playoff experience drive trader consensus.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump announced on April 19 that U.S. negotiators, led by Vice President JD Vance, will resume direct talks with Iran in Pakistan on Monday, amid escalating standoff over the Strait of Hormuz where Iran reversed its reopening pledge and the U.S. enforces a blockade for leverage. First-round Islamabad discussions collapsed April 12 after 21 hours, as Tehran rejected American demands for nuclear curbs, missile limits, sanctions relief, and regional security protocols. A fragile ceasefire persists post-U.S. strikes on Iranian nuclear sites and military assets, with Pakistan mediating a potential framework including a multi-year enrichment ban. Deep divides on permanent terms persist, with upcoming session key to de-escalation or breakdown.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders overwhelmingly price a 99.3% implied probability of no Federal Reserve rate change at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, driven by March 2026 CPI surging to 3.3% year-over-year—up sharply from 2.4%—fueled by war-related oil shocks, alongside nonfarm payrolls rebounding to +178,000 jobs and unemployment dipping to 4.3%. The Fed's March decision held the federal funds rate at 3.50%-3.75%, with dot-plot medians signaling just one cut later in 2026 amid resilient labor markets and elevated inflation risks. CME FedWatch Tool concurs at near-99% no-change odds. Realistic challenges include softer-than-expected April jobs data on May 2 or renewed core inflation cooling below 2.6%, potentially prompting earlier easing signals.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' George Russell and rookie Kimi Antonelli dominate trader consensus for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship, reflecting their 1-2 lead in standings after three Grands Prix with 63 and 72 points, respectively. Russell's Australian GP win from pole and Sprint victory in China underscore his experience edge over Antonelli's back-to-back triumphs in China and Japan, fueling his 41.5% implied probability amid an intra-team title battle. Antonelli's flawless rookie start at 30.4% highlights Mercedes' superior pace under new regulations, while Ferrari's Charles Leclerc (49 points, 6.1%) and others trail due to inconsistent podiums and weaker race pace, leaving little room for upsets barring mechanical issues or strategy shifts.

title: Republican Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President J.D. Vance trails Robert F. Kennedy Jr. closely at 39% implied probability in the 2028 Republican presidential nomination market, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 22%, reflecting trader consensus on Trump administration insiders dominating the early field amid term limits barring President Trump's third run. RFK Jr.'s surge to 49% follows his HHS confirmation and popular Make America Healthy Again reforms appealing to the base and independents, despite his prior denials of a bid, boosted by family speculation in early March. Vance holds as heir apparent via incumbency and CPAC straw poll leads, while Rubio gains on foreign policy wins. The tight race persists without primaries or Trump endorsements; separation could come from midterm results, early polling in Iowa and New Hampshire, or administration scandals.

title: Manchester City FC vs. Arsenal FC - More Markets
description: More markets for the Premier League game, scheduled for April 19 at 11:30 AM ET.

markets.question: Spread: Manchester City FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for April 19 at 11:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Manchester City FC" if Manchester City FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Arsenal FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's escalating injury crisis, highlighted by Gabriel Magalhães' fresh knee issue forcing him off in yesterday's FA Cup quarter-final loss to Southampton, has severely depleted their defensive options with William Saliba (ankle), Piero Hincapié (hamstring), and Mikel Merino (foot surgery) also sidelined, leaving Mikel Arteta short on center-backs for the April 19 Premier League title showdown at Etihad Stadium. Manchester City enter nearly fully fit after international breaks, boasting home advantage and momentum in the tight table race where Arsenal lead but face midweek Champions League demands against Sporting CP. Recent form shows Arsenal wobbling amid errors and absences, while City's squad depth positions them as trader consensus favorites despite historical tight head-to-heads.

eventMetadata.context_description: NVIDIA commands a dominant trader consensus at 99.2% implied probability of remaining the world's largest company by market capitalization through April's end, bolstered by its staggering $4.97 trillion valuation as of April 17—over $1 trillion ahead of Alphabet and Apple—fueled by unrelenting demand for its AI accelerators like the Blackwell platform amid explosive data center growth. Recent Magnificent 7 performance underscores this, with NVIDIA up 8% year-to-date in 2026 while peers show mixed results, reflecting sustained AI investment tailwinds and no credible reports of rivals closing the gap swiftly. Challenges could arise from a sudden AI hype cooldown triggering a NVIDIA sell-off, geopolitical trade disruptions impacting chip supply chains, or an unforeseen earnings beat propelling Alphabet or Apple into trillion-dollar surges before month-end close.

title: Counter-Strike: Team Falcons vs FURIA (BO3) - IEM Rio Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike 3rd place decider match between Team Falcons and FURIA in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 19 at 9:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Falcons" if Team Falcons win the match against FURIA.

This market will resolve to "FURIA" if FURIA win the match against Team Falcons.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map Handicap: FURIA (-1.5) vs Team Falcons (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike 3rd place decider match between Team Falcons and FURIA in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 19 at 9:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "FURIA" if FURIA wins 2 or more maps than Team Falcons in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Team Falcons". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Rounds?

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Falcons and FURIA clash in the IEM Rio 2026 third-place decider BO3 after semifinal losses to Spirit (0-2) and Vitality (0-2), respectively, on April 18. Falcons dominated Group A with wins over 3DMAX (2-0), Spirit (2-0), and Vitality (2-1), showcasing m0NESY and NiKo's rifling prowess, while FURIA advanced via Group B triumphs against Passion UA (2-0) and MOUZ (2-0), leveraging home-crowd energy in Rio. Recent head-to-head favors Falcons (2-1 wins in March BLAST Rotterdam and February PGL Cluj-Napoca), but FURIA's KSCERATO thrives on their pick Dust2. Falcons counter with Mirage preference; Nuke as decider. Top-5 rankings reflect balanced trader consensus on stylistic matchup potential.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 18.8% implied probability for the 2028 presidential winner, buoyed by his role as heir apparent in the Trump administration, recent book announcement on his Catholic conversion signaling early campaign groundwork, and a UMass Lowell poll from late March showing him leading California Gov. Gavin Newsom 33%-30% in a hypothetical matchup. Newsom trails closely at 16.9%, bolstered by Democratic primary polling strength despite billionaire tax backlash risks, while Secretary of State Marco Rubio sits at 11.3% after surging in a CPAC straw poll (35% to Vance's 53%). This tight field reflects pre-primary uncertainty, with 2026 midterms, Iran policy stances differentiating GOP contenders, and party endorsements poised to create separation amid volatile early polling.

title: Colombia Chamber of Representatives Election: 3rd Place
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Preliminary results from Colombia's March 8, 2026, legislative elections, certified by the Registraduría Nacional, position the Historic Pact for Colombia (PH) first with 42 seats in the Chamber of Representatives under proportional representation, followed by Centro Democrático (CD) at 30 seats, and Partido Liberal Colombiano (PLC) solidly third at 26 seats—well ahead of Partido Conservador's 19 and others like Alianza por Colombia's 14. Trader consensus at 90.5% on PLC reflects this decisive vote margin (PLC's 11.7% national share vs. competitors below 10%), with no recounts or disputes altering the top-three order in the six weeks since. Potential challenges include rare legal appeals over departmental allocations or special peace curules, though substantial gaps make shifts improbable ahead of congressional installation.

title: Dota 2: Xtreme Gaming vs Aurora (BO3) - PGL Wallachia Group Stage
description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Round 2 match between Xtreme Gaming and Aurora in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 19 at 3:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Xtreme Gaming" if Xtreme Gaming win the match against Aurora.

This market will resolve to "Aurora" if Aurora win the match against Xtreme Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 36.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming Round 2 match between Xtreme Gaming and Aurora in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 19 at 3:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 37 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

markets.question: Game 1: Ends in Daytime?
markets.description:
This market is about whether Game 1 ends during the daytime phase of the Dota 2 day/night cycle. In Dota 2, the day/night cycle alternates every 5 minutes (daytime runs from 0:00–5:00, 10:00–15:00, 20:00–25:00, and so on, while nighttime runs from 5:00–10:00, 15:00–20:00, 25:00–30:00, and so on).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the game-ending action (Ancient destruction or midgame forfeit) occurs during a daytime phase of the base in-game clock. This market will resolve to "No" if the game ends during a nighttime phase. Temporary nighttime effects caused by hero abilities (e.g., Night Stalker's Dark Ascension) do not affect resolution, only the base day/night cycle timer is considered.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Beat Roshan?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams kill Roshan at least once during Game 1. Roshan is a powerful neutral boss in Dota 2 that spawns at the start of the game and respawns 8–11 minutes after each death, dropping the Aegis of the Immortal and other valuable items.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Xtreme Gaming and Aurora each kill Roshan at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not kill Roshan during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via forfeit, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had killed Roshan prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Ends in Daytime?
markets.description:
This market is about whether Game 2 ends during the daytime phase of the Dota 2 day/night cycle. In Dota 2, the day/night cycle alternates every 5 minutes (daytime runs from 0:00–5:00, 10:00–15:00, 20:00–25:00, and so on, while nighttime runs from 5:00–10:00, 15:00–20:00, 25:00–30:00, and so on).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the game-ending action (Ancient destruction or midgame forfeit) occurs during a daytime phase of the base in-game clock. This market will resolve to "No" if the game ends during a nighttime phase. Temporary nighttime effects caused by hero abilities (e.g., Night Stalker's Dark Ascension) do not affect resolution, only the base day/night cycle timer is considered.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Beat Roshan?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams kill Roshan at least once during Game 2. Roshan is a powerful neutral boss in Dota 2 that spawns at the start of the game and respawns 8–11 minutes after each death, dropping the Aegis of the Immortal and other valuable items.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Xtreme Gaming and Aurora each kill Roshan at least once during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not kill Roshan during Game 2.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via forfeit, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had killed Roshan prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Destroy Barracks?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy barracks during Game 2. In Dota 2, each team has six barracks (a melee and ranged barracks in each of the three lanes), located behind the Tier 3 towers.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Xtreme Gaming and Aurora each destroy at least one enemy barracks (melee or ranged) during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy barracks.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via forfeit, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one barracks prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Any Player Ultra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves an Ultra Kill during Game 2. In Dota 2, an Ultra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy heroes in rapid succession without dying.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves an Ultra Kill during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves an Ultra Kill. A Rampage (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as an Ultra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via forfeit, this market will resolve based on whether an Ultra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Ultra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Any Player Rampage?
markets.description:

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 42.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming Round 2 match between Xtreme Gaming and Aurora in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 19 at 3:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 43 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

eventMetadata.context_description: In PGL Wallachia Season 8's Swiss group stage BO3 matchup, Xtreme Gaming enters with a 1-0 record after defeating Natus Vincere on Day 1, buoyed by a four-win streak in their last five series and #10 world ranking, while Aurora holds a 1-1 mark following mixed Round 1 results against South America Rejects. Head-to-head history favors XG slightly at 8-6 overall, though Aurora dominated their most recent clash 2-0 at ESL One Birmingham on March 23 amid patch 7.41b meta favoring aggressive cores like Lone Druid and Weaver. Stable rosters—no major changes or stand-ins beyond Aurora's prior V-Tune sub—position this as a stylistic battle where momentum and draft execution could dictate advancement toward double-elimination playoffs and the $1M prize pool.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Finland as the clear frontrunner at 36.6% implied probability for Eurovision 2026 victory, driven by the unique violin-pop fusion of Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's "Liekinheitin," a UMK national final winner that's dominating OGAE fan polls and preview events like Eurovision in Concert with its stage-ready spectacle appealing to both juries and televoters. France's Monroe surges to 11.4% on the momentum of her internally selected "Regarde!," whose catchy electro-pop has shortened odds amid recent betting shifts and strong Big Five pre-qualification buzz. Denmark holds third at 10.5% thanks to Søren Torpegaard Lund's showmanship in "Før Vi Går Hjem" post-Melodi Grand Prix win, bolstered by Nordic televote patterns. With all 35 entries released and semis looming May 12-14 in Vienna, preview performances and final OGAE tallies could spark further volatility.

title: LoL: Bilibili Gaming vs Anyone's Legend (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Bilibili Gaming and Anyone's Legend in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 19 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Bilibili Gaming" if Bilibili Gaming win the match against Anyone's Legend.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win the match against Bilibili Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Bilibili Gaming and Anyone's Legend each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Bilibili Gaming and Anyone's Legend each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bilibili Gaming enters this pivotal LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 against Anyone's Legend tied for second at 3-1, aiming to rebound from a 0-2 upset loss to group-leading Top Esports four days ago that halted their early dominance. AL, mired lower in standings after a 0-2 defeat to JDG last week, relies on veteran top laner Flandre and jungler Tarzan to challenge BLG's star-studded lineup of top Bin, mid Knight, jungler Xun, new ADC Viper—who has meshed seamlessly—and support ON. With top-four spots granting direct playoffs entry in the double round-robin format, recent head-to-heads show competitiveness, but BLG's superior macro play and prior 2-1 win over JDG fuel trader consensus on their edge amid stable rosters and current patch meta favoring aggressive early game tempo.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Bayern München's dominant 4-2 victory over VfB Stuttgart in Bundesliga Matchday 30 at Allianz Arena propelled the win outcome to 100% trader consensus, simultaneously clinching Bayern's 35th league title with four matches left. Pre-match favoritism stemmed from Bayern's atop-the-table position, impeccable home form at Allianz Arena, and lopsided head-to-head record—14 wins in the last 15 meetings against Stuttgart. The third-placed visitors mounted a brief second-half fightback but faltered against Bayern's firepower, including goals from Harry Kane and Raphael Guerreiro under Vincent Kompany. Realistic pre-game challenges like a Stuttgart clean sheet or red card upset evaporated amid Bayern's control, despite off-field ultras clashes.

title: Dota 2: Team Spirit vs Team Falcons (BO3) - PGL Wallachia Group Stage
description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Round 2 match between Team Spirit and Team Falcons in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 19 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Spirit" if Team Spirit win the match against Team Falcons.

This market will resolve to "Team Falcons" if Team Falcons win the match against Team Spirit.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

markets.question: Game Handicap: TS (-1.5) vs Team Falcons (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Round 2 match between Team Spirit and Team Falcons in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 19 at 8:20AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Spirit" if Team Spirit wins 2 or more games than Team Falcons in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Team Falcons". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

markets.question: Game 1: Ends in Daytime?
markets.description:
This market is about whether Game 1 ends during the daytime phase of the Dota 2 day/night cycle. In Dota 2, the day/night cycle alternates every 5 minutes (daytime runs from 0:00–5:00, 10:00–15:00, 20:00–25:00, and so on, while nighttime runs from 5:00–10:00, 15:00–20:00, 25:00–30:00, and so on).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the game-ending action (Ancient destruction or midgame forfeit) occurs during a daytime phase of the base in-game clock. This market will resolve to "No" if the game ends during a nighttime phase. Temporary nighttime effects caused by hero abilities (e.g., Night Stalker's Dark Ascension) do not affect resolution, only the base day/night cycle timer is considered.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Beat Roshan?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams kill Roshan at least once during Game 1. Roshan is a powerful neutral boss in Dota 2 that spawns at the start of the game and respawns 8–11 minutes after each death, dropping the Aegis of the Immortal and other valuable items.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team Spirit and Team Falcons each kill Roshan at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not kill Roshan during Game 1.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Spirit enters their PGL Wallachia Season 8 group stage BO3 against Team Falcons hampered by star offlaner Collapse's absence for personal reasons, with stand-in Batyuk stepping in—a significant roster adjustment that traders weigh heavily given Collapse's impact on laning and teamfights. Both squads sit at 1-0 after Round 1 wins, Spirit grinding a 2-1 thriller over Vici Gaming yesterday while Falcons advanced comfortably, positioning this matchup as pivotal for high-bracket advancement in the Swiss format toward playoffs. Falcons, ranked #4 globally with strong recent form including ESL One Birmingham successes, hold stylistic edges in mid-game execution over #8 Spirit's mixed results; head-to-head favors Falcons from Season 7. Current patch emphasizes flexible drafts amid communication concerns for Spirit.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: The US-Iran two-week ceasefire, effective since April 8 and set to expire April 22, has prevented direct military exchanges like airstrikes or missile attacks between Iran and Israel/US forces, but ongoing US naval blockade of Iranian ports—deemed a qualifying act of war—blocks the 14-day lull needed for market resolution. Iran reimposed its Strait of Hormuz closure yesterday, citing Israeli strikes in Lebanon as ceasefire violations, while Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei warned of naval retaliation. Pakistan-mediated talks stall over uranium enrichment limits and a framework agreement, with President Trump signaling a potential deal amid trader focus on de-escalation signals like the separate Israel-Hezbollah truce. No notable developments predate mid-March beyond the February 28 war onset.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and April 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for April 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 75.5% implied probability against Strait of Hormuz commercial traffic normalizing by April 30, driven by persistent US naval blockade enforcement since April 14 and Iranian restrictions capping daily transits at single digits—far below the pre-crisis average of 120–130 vessels. Recent shipping data from MarineTraffic and Bloomberg reveals ships halting or turning back amid elevated war risk insurance premiums and backlog of over 300 stranded vessels, despite a fragile ceasefire and Iran's partial reopenings. This geopolitical standoff sustains elevated energy market volatility, with traders awaiting diplomatic resolutions or blockade lifts as key catalysts before month-end, underscoring prediction markets' wisdom-of-crowds efficiency in pricing prolonged disruption risks.

title: Indian Premier League: Punjab Kings vs Lucknow Super Giants
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Punjab Kings and Lucknow Super Giants scheduled for April 19 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Punjab Kings vs Lucknow Super Giants - Who wins the toss?
markets.description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Punjab Kings and Lucknow Super Giants scheduled for 2026-04-19 in Indian Premier League.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Punjab Kings vs Lucknow Super Giants - Completed match?
markets.description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between Punjab Kings and Lucknow Super Giants scheduled for 2026-04-19 in Indian Premier League is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Punjab Kings enter this IPL clash at Mullanpur as the unbeaten table-toppers with five straight wins, fueled by explosive batting from Shreyas Iyer and Prabhsimran Singh, plus a stellar chasing record—nine victories in their last 11 pursuits since 2025—bolstered by Arshdeep Singh's death-over prowess. Lucknow Super Giants, seventh in standings after back-to-back defeats, grapple with batting inconsistencies despite Rishabh Pant's full recovery from an elbow knock and returns of Mayank Yadav and Mohsin Khan to their pace attack including Mohammed Shami. The fresh Mullanpur pitch promises high-scoring T20 action favoring batsmen, with toss winner likely to chase amid minimal dew risk and clear weather; LSG's historical 3-1 head-to-head edge offers upset potential if Pooran ignites the middle order.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Despite the 2026 US-Israel-Iran war's severe blows—including Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's February 28 assassination, strikes degrading military capabilities, and economic devastation—the Iranian regime retains firm control through IRGC loyalty and intensified domestic repression, including accelerated executions and internet blackouts. Trader consensus at 98.6% "No" reflects the April 8 ceasefire's hold, mediated by Pakistan, and ongoing nuclear negotiations over uranium suspension and Strait of Hormuz access, with no mass defections or uncontained uprisings since January's suppressed protests. Over the remaining 11 days to April 30, barriers like Basij militias and command continuity under reportedly wounded successor Mojtaba Khamenei underpin high confidence, though renewed airstrikes, Hormuz closure, or IRGC fracture could prompt rapid shifts.

title: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran diplomatic negotiations remain stalled after marathon face-to-face talks in Islamabad on April 11, led by US Vice President JD Vance, Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner opposite Iran's parliamentary speaker Mohammad Baqer Ghalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, failed to resolve disputes over Strait of Hormuz access, nuclear stockpile limits, and US naval blockade terms. A fragile two-week ceasefire expires within days, prompting Pakistani mediation for a second round potentially as soon as Monday, though Iran states it is reviewing US proposals without committing to a date or venue. Recent escalations, including Iran's Strait closure and US ship seizures, heighten risks, while traders weigh diplomatic breakthroughs against renewed military tensions before the deadline.
